title: markdown # Anjaneya Herbals - Spring Boot Backend ...

message.text:
markdown
# Anjaneya Herbals - Spring Boot Backend Requirements
I am building the backend for an e-commerce website named **Anjaneya Herbals**, an Ayurvedic products store specializing in traditional spices, herbal powders, and wellness products.
## Project Context (from existing React frontend)
The frontend is built with React + Vite + Tailwind CSS. It already has:
- A product listing with categories
- Product cards showing: [id](cci:1://file:///d:/AH/AH-new/Anjaneya-Herbals/src/components/Hero.jsx:34:2-34:92), `name`, `price`, `oldPrice`, `image`, `rating`, `reviews`, `sale`
- Categories: `Spices`, `Herbal Powders`, `Hair Care`, `Face Care`, `Body Care`, `Dry Fruits`
- User authentication flows (login/register pages exist)
- Cart functionality (context-based)
- Checkout flow ready for payment integration
---
## Backend Requirements
### Technology Stack
- **Framework**: Spring Boot 3.x (Java 17+)
- **Architecture**: Clean layered architecture (Controller → Service → Repository)
- **API Style**: RESTful with JSON responses
- **Scale**: Small catalog (~50-200 products), but production-ready design
---
## Functional Requirements
### 1. Product Management
```json
// Product Entity Fields (matching frontend)
{
  "id": "Long",
  "name": "String",
  "description": "String",
  "price": "BigDecimal",
  "oldPrice": "BigDecimal (nullable)",
  "imageUrl": "String",
  "rating": "Double (1.0-5.0)",
  "reviewCount": "Integer",
  "onSale": "Boolean",
  "stock": "Integer",
  "categoryId": "Long",
  "createdAt": "Timestamp",
  "updatedAt": "Timestamp"
}
2. Category Management
json
// Category Entity (matching frontend categories)
{
  "id": "Long",
  "name": "String", // Spices, Herbal Powders, Hair Care, Face Care, Body Care, Dry Fruits
  "slug": "String", // spices, herbal-powders, hair-care, etc.
  "imageUrl": "String",
  "description": "String",
  "displayOrder": "Integer"
}
3. User Management
Registration, Login, Logout
Profile management (name, email, phone, addresses)
Password reset flow
Role-based: CUSTOMER, ADMIN
4. Order Management
Create order from cart
Order history for users
Order status tracking: PENDING, CONFIRMED, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED
Admin order management
5. Cart Management
Add/remove/update cart items
Persist cart on server (for logged-in users)
6. Payment Gateway Readiness
Design for Razorpay integration (Indian audience primary)
Webhook endpoint for payment confirmation
Order status update on payment success/failure
Technical Requirements
Database
Recommendation: PostgreSQL (production-ready, free tier on Supabase/Railway/Render)
Design schema with proper relationships and indexes
Include seed data for the 6 categories
Security
JWT-based authentication (Access Token: 15min, Refresh Token: 7 days)
BCrypt password hashing
Role-based authorization (@PreAuthorize)
CORS configuration for frontend origin
API Endpoints Needed
# Public
GET    /api/products              - List products (with filters: category, search, sort)
GET    /api/products/{id}         - Product details
GET    /api/categories            - List categories
# Auth
POST   /api/auth/register         - User registration
POST   /api/auth/login            - User login (returns JWT)
POST   /api/auth/refresh          - Refresh access token
POST   /api/auth/logout           - Invalidate tokens
# Protected (Customer)
GET    /api/users/me              - Current user profile
PUT    /api/users/me              - Update profile
GET    /api/cart                  - Get cart
POST   /api/cart                  - Add to cart
DELETE /api/cart/{itemId}         - Remove from cart
POST   /api/orders                - Place order
GET    /api/orders                - Order history
# Protected (Admin)
POST   /api/admin/products        - Create product
PUT    /api/admin/products/{id}   - Update product
DELETE /api/admin/products/{id}   - Delete product
GET    /api/admin/orders          - All orders
PUT    /api/admin/orders/{id}     - Update order status
Image Storage Strategy
Store images in Cloudinary (free tier: 25GB)
OR use Supabase Storage (if using Supabase for DB)
Store only the URL in the database
Provide an admin endpoint for image upload that returns the CDN URL
Configuration
Use application.yml with profiles: dev, prod
Environment variables for secrets (DB credentials, JWT secret, Cloudinary keys)
Error Handling
Global exception handler with consistent error response format:
json
{
  "timestamp": "2024-12-23T18:00:00Z",
  "status": 400,
  "error": "Bad Request",
  "message": "Validation failed",
  "path": "/api/auth/register",
  "details": ["Email is required", "Password must be at least 8 characters"]
}
Deployment Recommendations (Low Cost)
Railway.app - $5/month for hobby tier, easy Spring Boot deploy
Render.com - Free tier with auto-sleep, PostgreSQL included
Fly.io - Free tier with 3 shared VMs
Deliverables Expected
Complete database schema (SQL or JPA entities)
Spring Boot project structure with all dependencies
All entity, repository, service, and controller classes
JWT security configuration
CORS configuration for React frontend
Seed data for categories
application.yml with dev/prod profiles
Dockerfile for deployment
README with setup instructions
Notes
Keep the solution clean, scalable, and cost-efficient
Frontend runs on http://localhost:5173 during development
Admin panel can be a separate route in the same frontend (no separate admin app needed)

Generate files and do according to this prompt
